Derivatives fitted by linear regression on rounded index values  |  Filter by min R² to exclude poor fits

This tool visualises the fully corrected aerodynamic wind-tunnel data from the AE4115 lab. All plotted values are _FINAL columns — the end result of the complete wall-correction pipeline (model-off tare, blockage, streamline-curvature, downwash, and tail-plane interference corrections). Use the tabs at the top to switch between the comparison view and the propOff explorer.

PropOn vs PropOff — Comparison

Overlays prop-on and prop-off data on the same chart, coloured by advance ratio J. The prop-off line is always shown as a dashed grey reference.

Controls

  • x-axis — choose what to sweep: AoA, AoS, or dR.
  • J — filter to a single advance ratio, or show all J values at once.
  • V (m/s) — filter to a single tunnel speed.
  • fix AoA / fix AoS / fix dR — lock a dimension to a specific value. When set to — all values are included and averaged together at each x point.
  • Actual x toggle — when OFF, the x-axis uses rounded grid values (AoA_round etc.) used for indexing. When ON, the axis shows the actual measured/corrected values (AoA, AoA_FINAL, V_FINAL). The grouping and averaging always uses rounded values regardless.

Metric tabs

Two groups separated by a gap:

  • CL CD CMpitch CYaw CMroll CMyaw CFt — final aerodynamic coefficients.
  • ΔCL sc ΔCD dw ΔCM sc ΔCM tail Δα dw Δα tail ε total ε wake ε slip Tc* CLw ref CD0 fit k fit R² fit — correction magnitudes and blockage factors.

Summary cards

For coefficient metrics (group 1), cards show the mean difference (prop-on minus prop-off) per J value across the currently filtered data. Green = prop-on higher, red = prop-on lower. Cards are hidden for correction metrics since a Δ of a correction does not have a clear sign convention.

Averaging behaviour

When a fix filter is set to —, all values in that dimension are averaged together at each x point. For example if fix AoS = — and x-axis = AoA, each chart point is the mean over all sideslip angles at that AoA. This can smooth out asymmetric effects — fix AoS = 0° for the cleanest symmetric case.

PropOff data is additionally averaged over repeat runs at the same index before being merged with prop-on data.

PropOff Explorer

Shows all individual propOff measurements (4000+ rows) without any aggregation. Useful for inspecting the full AoA sweep range, drag polars, and correction magnitudes across all test conditions.

Controls

  • x-axis — AoA, AoS, dR, dE, or V. More options than the comparison tab because the propOff dataset covers a much wider test matrix.
  • colour by — choose which dimension separates the lines: V, dE, dR, or AoS. Each unique value in that dimension gets its own coloured line.
  • V / fix AoA / fix AoS / fix dR / fix dE — same filtering logic as the comparison tab. Set to — to include all values (averaged at each x point).
  • Actual x toggle — same as comparison tab. For AoA this switches between AoA_round (grid) and AoA (raw measured). Note that AoA_FINAL is the fully corrected angle including streamline-curvature, downwash, and tail corrections — it can differ significantly from the raw AoA at high lift.

Metric tabs

Same two groups as the comparison tab, but with an extra correction metric Δα sc (streamline-curvature AoA increment) which is only meaningful for propOff since propOn uses a different CL source column.

Tip — inspecting the drag polar

Set x-axis = AoA, colour by = dE, fix AoS = 0°, and select CD or CD0 fit. This gives you the drag polar family for each elevator setting at symmetric flight.

Stability & Control

Extracts and plots aerodynamic stability and control derivatives fitted by linear regression from the measurement data. All derivatives are in per-degree units.

Plot types

  • Cnβ vs AoA — directional stability derivative ∂Cn/∂β. Fitted from CMyaw vs AoS at each (AoA, J, V, dR) condition. More negative = more stable. Prop-on shown solid by J, prop-off dashed by V.
  • Cyβ vs AoA — side-force stability ∂Cy/∂β. Always positive (side force in direction of sideslip).
  • Cn_dR vs AoA — rudder control power ∂Cn/∂δR. Fitted from CMyaw vs dR. More negative = more effective rudder.
  • Cy_dR vs AoA — side force due to rudder ∂Cy/∂δR.
  • Cr_dR vs AoA — roll coupling from rudder ∂Cl/∂δR (adverse/proverse yaw indicator).
  • Drag polar — CL vs CD scatter for all conditions. Overlays prop-on (coloured by J) and prop-off (grey). Shows how thrust shifts the polar.
  • Trim dR vs AoA — rudder deflection required to achieve CMyaw = 0 (directional trim) at each AoA and J, found by linear interpolation.
  • L/D at trim — lift-to-drag ratio at the trim dR condition. Shows the aerodynamic performance penalty from trimming.
  • CL at trim — lift coefficient at the trim condition.

Controls

  • dataset — show prop-on, prop-off, or both.
  • V — filter to a single tunnel speed.
  • fix dR — for Cnβ plots, restrict to a specific rudder angle (dR=0 gives the clean baseline).
  • fix dE — for prop-off plots, restrict to a specific elevator angle.
  • fix AoS — for dR-sweep plots (Cn_dR etc.), restrict to a specific sideslip. AoS=0 gives the symmetric case.
  • min R² — exclude fits with R² below the threshold. Default 0.80. Lower values include noisier fits; raise to 0.90+ for publication quality.

Interpretation notes

  • PropOn Cnβ is only available at AoA=2.5° (the only AoA with a full AoS sweep in the prop-on dataset). PropOff covers the full AoA range.
  • Trim dR values outside [−25°, 5°] are excluded as physically implausible (outside the rudder deflection range).
  • The drag polar scatter plot intentionally shows all raw points rather than averaged lines, so you can see the spread across conditions.

Correction Pipeline

All data has passed through the following correction sequence before being loaded into this tool. The _FINAL columns are the output of the last step.

PropOff sequence

  • Model-off tare subtraction
  • Drag polar fit  CD = CD0 + k·CL²
  • Solid blockage  ε_sb = 0.006406642
  • Wake blockage  ε_wb = S/(4C)·CD0 + 5S/(4C)·CDsep
  • Combined blockage correction  C_corr = C / (1+ε)²
  • Streamline-curvature correction
  • Downwash correction
  • Tail-plane interference correction

PropOn additional steps

  • BEM thrust separation  CT_bem(J) polynomial
  • Slipstream blockage  ε_ss = −(Tc*/2√(1+2Tc·))·(S_prop/C)
Blockage correction (velocity): V_corr = V / (1 + ε_total) Blockage correction (coeff): C_corr = C / (1 + ε_total)² CFt thrust: solid + wake only, never slipstream (circular)

Metric Glossary

Final coefficients

  • CL — lift coefficient (aerodynamic only after BEM separation for propOn)
  • CD — drag coefficient
  • CMpitch — pitching moment coefficient
  • CYaw — yaw force coefficient
  • CMroll — rolling moment coefficient
  • CMyaw — yawing moment coefficient
  • CFt — propeller thrust force coefficient (propOn only, solid+wake blockage corrected)

Correction magnitudes

  • ΔCL sc — CL increment from streamline-curvature correction
  • ΔCD dw — CD increment from downwash correction
  • ΔCM sc — CMpitch increment from streamline-curvature
  • ΔCM tail — CMpitch increment from tail-plane interference
  • Δα dw — AoA increment from downwash [deg]
  • Δα tail — AoA increment from tail correction [deg]
  • Δα sc — AoA increment from streamline-curvature [deg] (propOff only)

Blockage factors

  • ε total — total blockage factor (sb + wb [+ ss for propOn])
  • ε wake — wake blockage factor ε_wb
  • ε slip — slipstream blockage factor ε_ss (propOn only, negative by design)
  • Tc* — thrust loading T/(q·S_prop) driving slipstream blockage
  • CLw ref — tail-off reference lift used to drive SC, downwash, and tail corrections

Drag polar fit

  • CD0 fit — zero-lift drag from polar fit
  • k fit — induced drag factor from polar fit
  • R² fit — goodness of fit (values below ~0.85 indicate poor polar quality)
